So the reason we exist, right, in the broader content marketing evolution that we're seeing is, you know, when you think about what ChatGPT is doing to disrupt clicks to the website, when you think about how people are having like that first sales conversation in an LLM, what, you know, marketers have quickly realized is like, if you're not in the answer for your audience around the topics that they care about, it's almost like you don't exist.
And if you can get in the answer, then there's a big value to how much more likely AI search visits are to convert, right?
There's more intent, they spend three times as long, they're, you 4.4 times more likely, according to SEMrush, to convert.
And so Monograph is one of our customers, like we're helping clients out on the SEO and the GEO side, and we feel like, you know, they honestly benefit each other.
If you do it from an audience first perspective.
And so this is like an example of the traditional search compared to the way someone searches in a much more personalized way in an LLM.
And in both scenarios, right, we've got Monograph showing up near the top.
And the reason why we're able to do this consistently for our clients and really like create high quality editorial content at scale.
And it's not limited to editorial, but that's the foundation where we start.
It's because we've got, you know, these very sophisticated AI workflows, but we also have experts in the loop.
People that have run, you know, SEO at places like Fanatics and TripAdvisor, writers and editors who like built the G2.com website.

got certain threshold.
And if not, running the research again with, you know, different research questions or input.
And then also, you know, like the formatting, the comprehensiveness, the ability to create the content in your voice internally, in addition to refresh.
Refresh has been actually a way to generate super quick wins because even if the content is like a year old, right, you could refresh it, you could squeeze more juice out of it.
But that's, you know, the model and the world in which we're operating is the world where, you know, teams want to move fast, but they don't want to hire a lot.
They have used tools in the past and continue to do so, but there's a right time to value with tools.

There's the brittleness of drag and drop where you might have to replace things.
There's model.
People's changed from the very beginning.

like, it's, it just feels very timely.
